ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee beverage class; no specific 3-in-1 premix with creamer and reduced sugar subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers instant coffee powder; no dedicated 3-in-1 premix with creamer and reduced sugar code exists. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34718003 covers coffee as a substance; no specific 3-in-1 premix subtype exists. Closest defensible match used.

allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain casein)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard sachet (approximately 20 g) dissolved in 150–180 ml of hot water constitutes a typical single serving of this 3-in-1 instant coffee.

nutrient_source.title: 3 in 1 Flavoured Coffee (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo database record D09010009 for '3 in 1 flavoured coffee (less sugar)'. All core macros and secondary nutrients are as reported per 100 g of powder. Saturated fat estimated at 1.60 g/100 g based on typical non-dairy creamer palm-oil composition consistent with HPB category data; monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat, and detailed micronutrient data not reported by HPB for this entry and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal) / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific 3-in-1 less-sugar premix. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate source being maltodextrin from non-dairy creamer (GI ~85–110) blended with a small residual sugar fraction and diluted by the low total carbohydrate content per prepared serving. The analogous reference used is maltodextrin-based instant beverage mixes from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 2.36) / 100 ≈ 1.5, where carbohydrate per 20 g serving = 11.78 × 0.20 = 2.36 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (47 mg/100 g) and zero free sugar
hypertension.guidance: Sodium content is low at 47 mg per 100 g of powder (approximately 9.4 mg per 20 g serving), well below the threshold of concern for hypertension; however, caffeine in coffee can cause transient acute blood pressure elevation and intake should be limited to no more than two servings per day in individuals with uncontrolled hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI maltodextrin-based carbohydrate (11.78 g/100 g) with zero dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although labelled less sugar, the carbohydrate in this product is predominantly maltodextrin from the non-dairy creamer, which has a glycemic index of approximately 85–110 and causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this product and choose plain unsweetened black coffee instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from palm-oil-based non-dairy creamer (~1.60 g/100 g estimated)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The non-dairy creamer in this product is typically palm-oil-based, contributing approximately 1.60 g of saturated fat per 100 g of powder; while the absolute amount per serving (approximately 0.32 g per 20 g sachet) is modest, regular daily consumption adds to cumulative saturated fat intake and may contribute to LDL elevation in individuals with cardiovascular disease risk, who should prefer plain black coffee.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 3-in-1 Flavoured Instant Coffee (Less Sugar) is an ultra-processed instant beverage powder popular in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. Each sachet contains a pre-blended mixture of instant coffee, non-dairy creamer (typically palm-oil-based), a reduced quantity of sugar, and additional flavourings. The product is prepared by dissolving one sachet (approximately 20 g) in 150–180 ml of hot water, yielding a sweet, creamy, mildly coffee-flavoured drink. Per 100 g of powder, the product provides 70 kcal of energy, 0.23 g protein, 2.48 g fat (of which approximately 1.60 g is saturated fat from the palm-oil creamer), 11.78 g carbohydrate, 0 g free sugar (as reported by HPB), 0 g dietary fibre, and 47 mg sodium. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the carbohydrate content is predominantly maltodextrin from the non-dairy creamer, a high-glycemic-index refined starch (GI ~85–110) that can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation comparable to or exceeding that of sucrose. This makes the product unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or insulin resistance. The palm-oil-based creamer contributes pro-inflammatory saturated fat and is a concern for cardiovascular health with regular consumption. Sodium is low and the glycemic load per standard serving is modest at approximately 1.5, making hypertension risk low. The product is commonly consumed at breakfast in Singapore and is widely available in convenience stores and supermarkets. It is halal-certified in most commercial formulations. Plain unsweetened black coffee is the preferred alternative for individuals with metabolic or cardiovascular health concerns.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a sachet of 3-in-1 less sugar coffee?
Is premix coffee bad for blood sugar?
What is the GI of instant 3-in-1 coffee?
Can I drink 3-in-1 coffee if I have hypertension?
Does less sugar coffee have maltodextrin?
What is in non-dairy creamer in instant coffee?
Is premix coffee high in sodium?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (record D09010009), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. The match is exact. Saturated fat is estimated from typical non-dairy creamer composition consistent with HPB category data and is documented as an adaptation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008, clearly documented.
notes: sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo record D09010009; the product is labelled less sugar and HPB reports 0.0 g free sugar per 100 g, consistent with the formulation using maltodextrin rather than sucrose as the primary carbohydrate. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source — instant coffee premix with non-dairy creamer contains no fibre-contributing ingredients. calcium_mg and iron_mg are confirmed zero per HPB source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this specific 3-in-1 less sugar premix are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry D09010009 as of 2026-04-16. saturated_fat_g of 1.60 g/100 g is an adaptation estimated from typical palm-oil-based non-dairy creamer composition consistent with HPB category-level data; this is not directly reported in the HPB record and is documented as an adapted value. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean cuisine category: English (food_name), Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16.
